events.title: 'Pre-existing vulnerabilities: Venezuela already experiencing economic turmoil, displacement crisis'
events.description: Genie Godula is pleased to welcome Elinor Raikes. Vice President and Head of Program Delivery at the International Rescue Committee. According to Raikes, earthquakes expose and amplify the vulnerabilities that already exist beneath the surface of a society. She suggests that the devastation unfolding in Venezuela is not solely the consequence of seismic forces, but the collision of a major natural disaster exacerbated by economic collapse, displacement, weakened public services, and shrinking humanitarian funding. Rakes warns the earthquake is not a short-term emergency but rather the beginning of a prolonged humanitarian crisis. She emphasises the logistical obstacles confronting aid agencies, the importance of coordination between international and local organisations, and the reality that recovery will be measured in months rather than weeks.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ 'Pre-existing vulnerabilities: Venezuela already experiencing economic turmoil, displacement crisis'

events.title: Weather tracker: Heatwave expected to bring Germany and Poland to 40C
events.description: Austrian Grand Prix declares heat hazard as dramatic rise in temperatures forecast in central and northern Europe Temperatures are forecast to rise dramatically in parts of central and northern Europe this weekend as the intense heatwave continues . In Germany and Poland, highs up to or exceeding 40C (104F) are expected on both Saturday and Sunday, days after swathes of France experienced similar extreme temperatures. The Austrian Grand Prix, taking place this weekend in Spielberg, has declared a heat hazard , the first race to do so this season. Temperatures are expected to rise into the low 30s celsius during the race, almost 10C warmer than usual for the venue. Across Europe, the intense heat has led to severe thunderstorms. In the Sverdlovsk region of central Russia, a strong tornado was reported on the evening of 22 June. Rated a 3 out of 5 on the international Fujita scale, the twister injured 16 people and destroyed or damaged about 100 homes and businesses in the town of Kushva. Emergency services have been working on restoring power to the town. Tornadoes are not unheard of in Russia but are rare. Forecasters say they may become more common in future as the climate breaks down. Storm warnings remained in effect in the southern part of the country on Friday. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Weather tracker: Heatwave expected to bring Germany and Poland to 40C

events.title: Families of two football players killed and injured in Venezuela earthquakes
events.description: Lucas Trejo of Argentina loses wife and two children while Héctor Bello’s partner dies saving their daughter ‘Critical hours’ in Venezuela earthquake rescue operation – latest updates Family members of two professional footballers are among those killed by the twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela in a catastrophe that has claimed at least 1,450 lives . Among the reported victims are the wife and two children of Lucas Trejo, an Argentinian footballer signed to Club Sport Marítimo of La Guaira. He searched through rubble in the coastal city for his wife, Yanina, and their children, Aaron and Ainhoa, for three days before their bodies were recovered by rescue workers. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Families of two football players killed and injured in Venezuela earthquakes

events.title: Venezuela earthquake: father and son found alive in rubble after four days as death toll nears 1,500
events.description: President says ‘we always hold onto hope’ as discovery of earthquake survivors spurs fresh search efforts despite dwindling chances of survival Mortuary in Caracas ‘overwhelmed’ as Venezuela struggles to respond after earthquakes A man and his teenage son were found alive under the rubble in Venezuela on Sunday, in a town about 40km north of the capital Caracas, AFP journalists reported, as the death toll from last week’s twin earthquakes passed 1,450. The discovery of survivors in Caraballeda was made by French and American rescue teams nearly four days after back-to-back quakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 struck, completely destroying nearly 200 buildings in the area. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Venezuela earthquake: father and son found alive in rubble after four days as death toll nears 1,500

events.title: Venezuelan earthquakes test Trump’s new western hemisphere policy after gutting of USAID
events.description: Marco Rubio is scrambling to provide effective disaster response to country whose president US deposed in January This week’s dual earthquakes in Venezuela are a test for the new era of American power in the western hemisphere, as the Trump administration scrambles to provide an effective disaster response mission to a country that it now calls an ally in Latin America, after a US special forces raid in January deposed the country’s strongman leader, Nicolás Maduro. The US is marshalling what secretary of state Marco Rubio called a “big, fast, effective” and “whole-of-government” response as the state department sent three specialised urban search and rescue teams and pledged a $150m assistance fund that one former disaster relief expert called the largest he had seen within 24 hours of an incident. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Venezuelan earthquakes test Trump’s new western hemisphere policy after gutting of USAID

Demonstrators accuse them, without evidence, of stealing jobs - despite them representing about 4% of the population -, collapsing public services and #crime.

events.title: French hospitals 'better prepared': 'Important lessons learned from deadly 2003 heatwave'
events.description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Dr. Antoine Flahault, Epidemiologist, Researcher, and Professor at Université Paris Cité - Hospital Bichat. France's latest heatwave has once again exposed healthcare systems were largely designed for another era. While the immediate focus often falls on soaring temperatures and overcrowded emergency departments, Dr. Flahault argues that the deeper issue is institutional adaptation. He suggests that France has learned important lessons since the deadly 2003 heatwave, with hospitals adopting better clinical practices and emergency planning. Yet as heatwaves become more frequent and intense, protecting vulnerable populations will increasingly require structural investment, particularly widespread access to air conditioning in hospitals, care homes, schools and, for those most at risk, private homes.

events.title: Weather tracker: North-west US hit by snow ahead of eastern heatwave
events.description: Western states experience unseasonally low temperatures but New York and Washington DC could reach 40C by end of week Unseasonal snow has fallen in some parts of the US, while other parts of the country brace for a heatwave this week. A strong cold front spread into the western US from the northern Pacific over the weekend, bringing an abrupt change in conditions to a region that had been experiencing high summer temperatures amid drought. Temperatures from the Canadian border to California have widely been 5-10C below the norm since Friday, and more than 10C below in some parts farther north. The pattern is expected to remain for much of the coming week. Continue reading...

events.title: London artist Georgia Semple explores tension between perception and reality
events.description: Her paintings are rich, layered works that feature distorted or warped figures – something that Georgia Semple says represents the "loss of perception" that one experiences throughout one's life. Speaking to FRANCE 24, the London-based artist explains how themes of faith and addiction as well as her Guyanese heritage are expressed in her thought-provoking works. She says she hopes her paintings might prompt viewers to question "their own truths" or "how faith might show up in their lives".

events.title: Jazz à Vienne celebrates late Miles Davis for 45th edition
events.description: In this edition of arts24, Marjorie Hache is in the town of Vienne in southeastern France, where the 45th edition of "Jazz à Vienne" is taking place. Headliners include the likes of Jon Batiste, Marcus Miller, Buena Vista Social Club and Jeff Mills. The festival is also shining a spotlight on late jazz legend Miles Davis, 100 years since his birth. The American musician played at the festival four times during his life and left a lasting mark on the town. Marjorie also shares one of her festival highlights: Cameroonian band Akutuk's concert in a swimming pool in an ode to their ancestral links with water. She also chats with New Orleans trumpeter James Andrews, who is transforming the streets of Vienne into a giant parade.

events.title: Bruce Lehrmann loses bid to get footage from documentary featuring Brittany Higgins ahead of rape trial
events.description: Judge says ‘no legitimate forensic purpose’ to former Liberal staffer’s subpoena requesting footage, ahead of his Queensland trial Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Former political staffer Bruce Lehrmann has lost a legal bid to access copies of all footage from a documentary featuring Brittany Higgins. Lehrmann had subpoenaed the producers of the documentary Silenced and signalled a possible attempt to ban its broadcast before his Queensland trial for rape. Continue reading...

events.title: Australia to double potential fines over child social media accounts
events.description: Australia plans to double potential fines for social media platforms, including Facebook and Instagram, who fail to prevent Australian children from holding accounts.

events.title: Poland orders Swedish submarines to bulk up navy
events.description: Poland has placed an order for three Swedish submarines in an effort to step up defense capabilities. The NATO member is modernizing its navy in the face of Russian aggression at its borders.

events.title: EV myths muddy the road to electrification
events.description: Claims that electric vehicle batteries are tainted by exploitative mineral supply chains are discrediting non-fossil cars as electrified road transport booms.
events.ticker_label: 💥 EV myths muddy the road to electrification

events.title: UK-Africa relations: Starmer's 'reset' that never delivered
events.description: Back in 2024 British Prime Minister Keir Starmer promised a fresh UK–Africa partnership. Two years later, his resignation leaves unfinished ambitions, scaled-back aid and questions about Britain's long-term commitment.

events.title: US-India trade deal: Inside the key sticking points
events.description: Both India and the US have signaled that the first phase of a landmark trade deal is close. DW looks at the main challenges in getting a deal past the finish line.

events.title: Can Thailand, Cambodia unlock massive oil and gas reserves?
events.description: An estimated $300 billion in oil and gas reserves is sitting under overlapping territory in the Gulf of Thailand. Cooperation is complicated by political tensions over a separate land border dispute.

events.title: In Slovenia, Pride meets growing support — and hostility
events.description: One of the first steps taken by Slovenia's new right-wing government in June was to remove the rainbow flag outside the Culture Ministry. As Pride Month draws to a close, DW surveys the situation of the LGBTQ+ community.

events.title: EIB announces largest-ever loan: €3 billion to Airbus
events.description: The European Investment Bank (EIB) has said the loan, the largest in its history, is aimed at helping boost the EU planemaker's research efforts. The money will be directed to commercial and defense projects.

events.title: Australia-Vanuatu deal bars foreign bases in island nation
events.description: Australia and Vanuatu have signed a deal seen as a bid to check China's growing security presence in the region. But an economic deal between Vanuatu and China is still in the works.

events.title: Pakistan's Geo News apologizes for Muharram broadcast
events.description: The broadcaster Geo News was handed a 15-day suspension for a documentary that was "liable to hurt the religious sentiments of viewers," Pakistan's media watchdog said.

events.title: Middle East live: Trump says Iran talks to be held in Qatar, but Tehran denies it
events.description: No description available.

events.title: Man charged with murder after girl's body found in suitcase in Thailand
events.description: An Australian man has been charged with the murder of a teenage girl in Thailand after her body was found in a suitcase by police on Saturday.

events.title: Iraqi officials, including lawmakers, arrested on corruption charges
events.description: Iraqi political officials have been arrested on corruption charges

events.title: Europe's record heat has overwhelmed Paris mortuaries and left families in distress
events.description: A record-breaking heat wave has overwhelmed mortuaries in Paris, leaving funeral directors struggling to find space for bodies

events.title: Ugandan army chief orders the closure of a major news platform
events.description: Uganda's military chief and son of President Yoweri Museveni has ordered the shutdown of a major news organization

events.title: A Chinese dissident recounts his perilous dinghy escape to South Korea and how he got to Canada
events.description: Chinese dissident Dong Guangping has finally reached Canada after a perilous escape from China

events.title: Australia to double potential fines for Facebook and Instagram over child social media accounts
events.description: Australia plans to double potential fines for social media platforms, including Facebook and Instagram, who fail to prevent Australian children from holding accounts

events.title: China imposes export controls on 40 Japanese entities as tensions with Tokyo rise
events.description: China has imposed new export controls on 40 Japanese entities, accusing them of contributing to Japan’s “remilitarization.”

events.title: Malaysia extends search for MH370 for another year, keeping families’ hopes alive
events.description: Malaysia has extended its contract with Ocean Infinity for another year to search for wreckage of Malaysia Airlines flight 370

events.title: Milei's top aide and Cabinet chief resigns over spiraling corruption scandal
events.description: Argentine President Javier Milei’s Cabinet chief and close ally, Manuel Adorni, has resigned following a corruption scandal that has roiled the government, undermining its flagship campaign pledge to stamp out endemic graft in the political elite

events.title: A long-awaited Australia-Vanuatu pact blocks China from building a military base
events.description: Australia and Vanuatu have signed a long-awaited bilateral security and economic treaty that prevents China creating a military base on the South Pacific island nation

events.title: Guterres urges renewed commitment to multilateralism on UN Charter Day
events.description: Secretary-General António Guterres and General Assembly President Annalena Baerbock reaffirmed the principles outlined in the UN Charter and called for a renewed commitment to multilateralism at a General Assembly meeting on Friday marking the Charter’s 81st anniversary.

events.title: Security Council extends critical stabilisation force in Syria
events.description: The 15-member Security Council unanimously adopted a draft resolution on Thursday renewing the mandate of the UN Disengagement Observer Force (UNDOF) for six months.

events.title: Fox News Campus Radicals Newsletter: Parents take schools to court, drag queen school board member arrested
events.description: Stay up to date with the Fox News Campus Radicals newsletter.

events.title: Man dead, another critical after double stabbing at Brooklyn park as police detain person of interest: NYPD
events.description: A 21-year-old man died and a 30-year-old remains in critical condition after a double stabbing at Dyker Beach Park in Brooklyn on Saturday afternoon.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Man dead, another critical after double stabbing at Brooklyn park as police detain person of interest: NYPD

events.title: Parents of 7-year-old who died weighing 255 pounds charged with murder in suspected neglect case
events.description: Parents of a 7-year-old boy who died weighing 255 pounds face murder charges after prosecutors say the child was bedridden and living in filth.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Parents of 7-year-old who died weighing 255 pounds charged with murder in suspected neglect case

events.title: Women accuse Pearadise founder of preying on them by turning ‘safe space’ into mansion of manipulation
events.description: A viral body positivity community called Pearadise faces allegations of predatory behavior by its founder, sparking a legal battle and a new docuseries.

events.title: As America turns 250, a rare 1790 exchange between Washington and Bishop Carroll takes on new relevance
events.description: A 1790 letter from Bishop John Carroll to George Washington sought reassurance that Catholics would be equal citizens in the new American republic.

events.title: Farage says mass migration has changed the UK ‘literally beyond recognition,’ believes party can win election
events.description: Nigel Farage calls Britain's political system "completely broken" after Starmer's resignation and says Reform UK would have every chance of winning.
